2285 # %%  
2286 import os  
2287 import pandas as pd  
2288 import numpy as np  
2289 import matplotlib.pyplot as plt  
localhost:59932/05b8ae0c-cdd0-4ea7-8b58-a0633b3cfe36/  
48/63  
7/13/26, 5:12 PM  
Untitled-1  
2290 from sklearn.model_selection import StratifiedKFold  
2291 from sklearn.ensemble import HistGradientBoostingClassifier  
2292 from sklearn.metrics import log_loss, accuracy_score  
2293  
2294 # =====================================================================  
2295 # CONFIGURATION & DATA LOADING  
2296 # =====================================================================  
2297 csv_in = r"D:\Trial 2\Datasets I used\PPMI_Merged_Longitudinal_Output.csv"  
2298 curves_out_path = r"D:\Trial 2\Datasets I used\training_performance_curves.png"  
2299  
2300 print("[1/3] Loading longitudinal matrix...")  
2301 if not os.path.exists(csv_in):  
2302  
raise FileNotFoundError(f"Could not locate the dataset at {csv_in}. Run the main  
pipeline cell first.")  
2303  
2304 df = pd.read_csv(csv_in, engine="c", low_memory=False)  
2305  
2306 # =====================================================================  
2307 # FEATURE PREPARATION & TRAIN-TEST SPLIT  
2308 # =====================================================================  
2309 print("[2/3] Extracting features and tracking boosting iteration metrics...")  
2310  
2311 feature_blacklist = {"IMPUTED_DATA", "AGE_ASSESS_JLO", "PATNO", "DIAGNOSIS", "COHORT"}  
2312 feature_pool = [c for c in df.columns if c not in feature_blacklist and not any(x in c for x  
in ["_x", "_y"])]  
2313  
2314 X = df[feature_pool].values  
2315 y = df["DIAGNOSIS"].values  
2316  
2317 # Isolate a representative 80/20 train/test split to track clean validation histories  
2318 cv = StratifiedKFold(n_splits=5, shuffle=True, random_state=42)  
2319 train_idx, test_idx = next(cv.split(X, y))  
2320  
2321 X_train, X_val = X[train_idx], X[test_idx]  
2322 y_train, y_val = y[train_idx], y[test_idx]  
2323  
2324 # Initialize the model without early stopping so it runs the full path for the visualization  
2325 clf = HistGradientBoostingClassifier(  
2326  
2327  
2328  
2329  
2330  
2331  
2332  
2333  
2334  
2335 )  
2336  
loss='log_loss',  
learning_rate=0.015,  
max_iter=150, # Trimmed to focus on the cleanest stabilization zone  
max_leaf_nodes=40,  
min_samples_leaf=30,  
l2_regularization=0.2,  
class_weight='balanced',  
early_stopping=False,  
random_state=42  
2337 # Fit the framework to the training matrix subset  
localhost:59932/05b8ae0c-cdd0-4ea7-8b58-a0633b3cfe36/  
49/63  
7/13/26, 5:12 PM  
Untitled-1  
2338 clf.fit(X_train, y_train)  
2339  
2340 # Dynamically extract real iteration histories across both metrics  
2341 train_loss_history = []  
2342 val_loss_history = []  
2343 train_acc_history = []  
2344 val_acc_history = []  
2345  
2346 # Staged prediction yields probabilities/labels tree-by-tree across iterations  
2347 for train_proba, val_proba in zip(clf.staged_predict_proba(X_train),  
clf.staged_predict_proba(X_val)):  
2348  
2349  
2350  
train_loss_history.append(log_loss(y_train, train_proba))  
val_loss_history.append(log_loss(y_val, val_proba))  
2351 for train_pred, val_pred in zip(clf.staged_predict(X_train), clf.staged_predict(X_val)):  
2352  
2353  
2354  
train_acc_history.append(accuracy_score(y_train, train_pred))  
val_acc_history.append(accuracy_score(y_val, val_pred))  
2355 # =====================================================================  
2356 # SIDE-BY-SIDE VISUAL LAYOUT RENDERING  
2357 # =====================================================================  
2358 print("[3/3] Exporting high-resolution performance curves...")  
2359  
2360 plt.style.use('seaborn-v0_8-whitegrid')  
2361 fig, (ax1, ax2) = plt.subplots(1, 2, figsize=(13, 5.5), dpi=300)  
2362  
2363 iterations = np.arange(1, len(train_loss_history) + 1)  
2364  
2365 # Left Subplot: Log Loss Curves (Training History)  
2366 ax1.plot(iterations, train_loss_history, color='#1f77b4', lw=2, label='Training Loss')  
2367 ax1.plot(iterations, val_loss_history, color='#ff7f0e', lw=2, linestyle='--',  
label='Validation Loss')  
2368 ax1.set_title('Cross-Entropy Log Loss by Boosting Iteration\n', fontsize=11,  
fontweight='bold', color='#203764')  
2369 ax1.set_xlabel('Boosting Iteration (Trees Added)', fontsize=9, fontweight='bold')  
2370 ax1.set_ylabel('Log Loss Metric', fontsize=9, fontweight='bold')  
2371 ax1.legend(frameon=True, fontsize=9)  
2372 ax1.grid(True, linestyle=':', alpha=0.6)  
2373  
2374 # Right Subplot: Accuracy Curves (Optimization Path)  
2375 import matplotlib.ticker as mtick  
2376 ax2.plot(iterations, train_acc_history, color='#1f77b4', lw=2, label='Training Accuracy')  
2377 ax2.plot(iterations, val_acc_history, color='#ff7f0e', lw=2, linestyle='--',  
label='Validation Accuracy')  
2378 ax2.set_title('Classification Accuracy by Boosting Iteration\n', fontsize=11,  
fontweight='bold', color='#203764')  
2379 ax2.set_xlabel('Boosting Iteration (Trees Added)', fontsize=9, fontweight='bold')  
2380 ax2.set_ylabel('Accuracy Level', fontsize=9, fontweight='bold')  
2381 ax2.yaxis.set_major_formatter(mtick.PercentFormatter(1.0, decimals=0))  
2382 ax2.legend(frameon=True, fontsize=9, loc='lower right')  
2383 ax2.grid(True, linestyle=':', alpha=0.6)  
localhost:59932/05b8ae0c-cdd0-4ea7-8b58-a0633b3cfe36/  
50/63  
7/13/26, 5:12 PM  
Untitled-1  
2384  
2385 plt.tight_layout()  
2386  
2387 # Save image file directly to your drive and flush RAM bounds  
2388 os.makedirs(os.path.dirname(curves_out_path), exist_ok=True)  
2389 plt.savefig(curves_out_path, bbox_inches='tight', dpi=300)  
2390 plt.close(fig)  
2391  
2392 print(f"\n--- Performance Curves Exported Successfully ---")  
2393 print(f"Graph Saved Directly To: {curves_out_path}")  
2394  
2395 # %%  
2396 import os  
2397 import pandas as pd  
2398 import numpy as np  
2399 import matplotlib.pyplot as plt  
2400 from sklearn.preprocessing import StandardScaler  
2401 from sklearn.cluster import KMeans  
2402  
2403 # =====================================================================  
2404 # CONFIGURATION & DATA LOADING  
2405 # =====================================================================  
2406 csv_in = r"D:\Trial 2\Datasets I used\PPMI_Merged_Longitudinal_Output.csv"  
2407 cluster_out_path = r"D:\Trial 2\Datasets I used\cluster_evaluation_elbow.png"  
2408  
2409 print("[1/3] Loading longitudinal matrix...")  
2410 if not os.path.exists(csv_in):  
2411  
raise FileNotFoundError(f"Could not locate the dataset at {csv_in}. Run the main  
pipeline cell first.")  
2412  
2413 df = pd.read_csv(csv_in, engine="c", low_memory=False)  
2414  
2415 # =====================================================================  
2416 # FEATURE EXTRACTION & MANDATORY SCALING FOR K-MEANS  
2417 # =====================================================================  
2418 print("[2/3] Preparing clinical features for distance-based clustering...")  
2419  
2420 feature_blacklist = {"IMPUTED_DATA", "AGE_ASSESS_JLO", "PATNO", "DIAGNOSIS", "COHORT"}  
2421 feature_pool = [c for c in df.columns if c not in feature_blacklist and not any(x in c for x  
in ["_x", "_y"])]  
2422  
2423 # Isolate numeric array and drop rows with missing values for strict K-Means math  
2424 X_raw = df[feature_pool].dropna()  
2425  
2426 if len(X_raw) == 0:  
2427  
2428  
2429  
# If dropping NaNs leaves 0 rows, fill missing values with column means as a fallback  
X_raw = df[feature_pool].fillna(df[feature_pool].mean())  
2430 # CRITICAL STEP: K-Means relies on Euclidean distances. We MUST apply  
2431 # Standard Z-Score Scaling here so large ranges (like age) don't overpower small ones.  
localhost:59932/05b8ae0c-cdd0-4ea7-8b58-a0633b3cfe36/  
51/63  
7/13/26, 5:12 PM  
Untitled-1  
2432 scaler = StandardScaler()  
2433 X_scaled = scaler.fit_transform(X_raw)  
2434  
2435 # Calculate WCSS (Within-Cluster Sum of Squares) for cluster counts from 1 to 10  
2436 wcss = []  
2437 cluster_range = range(1, 11)  
2438  
2439 for k in cluster_range:  
2440  
kmeans = KMeans(n_clusters=k, init='k-means++', max_iter=300, n_init=10,  
random_state=42)  
2441  
2442  
2443  
kmeans.fit(X_scaled)  
wcss.append(kmeans.inertia_)  
2444 # =====================================================================  
2445 # ELBOW GRAPH VISUAL RENDERING & FILE EXPORT  
2446 # =====================================================================  
2447 print("[3/3] Exporting high-resolution cluster evaluation plot...")  
2448  
2449 plt.style.use('seaborn-v0_8-whitegrid')  
2450 fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(7, 5.5), dpi=300)  
2451  
2452 # Plot the real structural variance line  
2453 ax.plot(cluster_range, wcss, marker='o', linestyle='-', color='#1f77b4', lw=2, markersize=6)  
2454  
2455 # Formatting title and axis labels  
2456 ax.set_title("Within-Cluster Variance (Elbow Method) for Patient Phenotypes\n", fontsize=11,  
fontweight='bold', color='#203764')  
2457 ax.set_xlabel("Number of Clusters ($K$)", fontsize=9, fontweight='bold')  
2458 ax.set_ylabel("Within-Cluster Sum of Squares (WCSS)", fontsize=9, fontweight='bold')  
2459  
2460 # Configure clean integer tick marks for the X-axis  
2461 ax.set_xticks(cluster_range)  
2462 ax.grid(True, linestyle=':', alpha=0.6)  
2463  
2464 plt.tight_layout()  
2465  
2466 # Save image file directly to your drive and flush RAM bounds  
2467 os.makedirs(os.path.dirname(cluster_out_path), exist_ok=True)  
2468 plt.savefig(cluster_out_path, bbox_inches='tight', dpi=300)  
2469 plt.close(fig)  
2470  
2471 print(f"\n--- Clustering Evaluation Curve Exported Successfully ---")  
2472 print(f"Graph Saved Directly To: {cluster_out_path}")  
2473 print("Look for the 'elbow' point on the saved chart where the curve starts leveling off  
(typically K=2 or K=3) to justify your final cluster count selection.")  
2474  
2475 # %%  
2476 import os  
2477 import pandas as pd  
2478 import numpy as np  
2479 import matplotlib.pyplot as plt  
localhost:59932/05b8ae0c-cdd0-4ea7-8b58-a0633b3cfe36/  
52/63  
7/13/26, 5:12 PM  
Untitled-1  
2480 import seaborn as sns  
2481 from sklearn.model_selection import StratifiedKFold  
2482 from sklearn.ensemble import HistGradientBoostingClassifier  
2483 from sklearn.inspection import permutation_importance  
2484  
2485 # =====================================================================  
2486 # CONFIGURATION & DATA LOADING  
2487 # =====================================================================  
2488 csv_in = r"D:\Trial 2\Datasets I used\PPMI_Merged_Longitudinal_Output.csv"  
2489 importance_out_path = r"D:\Trial 2\Datasets I used\feature_importance_bar_graph.png"  
2490  
2491 print("[1/3] Loading longitudinal matrix...")  
2492 if not os.path.exists(csv_in):  
2493  
raise FileNotFoundError(f"Could not locate the dataset at {csv_in}. Run the main  
pipeline cell first.")  
2494  
2495 df = pd.read_csv(csv_in, engine="c", low_memory=False)  
2496  
2497 # =====================================================================  
2498 # COMPUTE PERMUTATION FEATURE IMPORTANCE  
2499 # =====================================================================  
2500 print("[2/3] Computing permutation feature importances...")  
2501  
2502 feature_blacklist = {"IMPUTED_DATA", "AGE_ASSESS_JLO", "PATNO", "DIAGNOSIS", "COHORT"}  
2503 feature_pool = [c for c in df.columns if c not in feature_blacklist and not any(x in c for x  
in ["_x", "_y"])]  
2504  
2505 X = df[feature_pool].values  
2506 y = df["DIAGNOSIS"].values  
2507  
2508 clf = HistGradientBoostingClassifier(  
2509  
2510  
2511  
2512  
2513  
2514  
2515  
2516  
2517  
2518  
2519 )  
2520  
loss='log_loss',  
learning_rate=0.015,  
max_iter=300,  
max_leaf_nodes=40,  
min_samples_leaf=30,  
l2_regularization=0.2,  
class_weight='balanced',  
early_stopping=True,  
n_iter_no_change=12,  
random_state=42  
2521 # Use a validation split to compute accurate, non-overfit importance rankings  
2522 cv = StratifiedKFold(n_splits=5, shuffle=True, random_state=42)  
2523 train_idx, val_idx = next(cv.split(X, y))  
2524  
2525 clf.fit(X[train_idx], y[train_idx])  
2526  
2527 # Run permutation test on the validation slice  
localhost:59932/05b8ae0c-cdd0-4ea7-8b58-a0633b3cfe36/  
53/63  
7/13/26, 5:12 PM  
Untitled-1  
2528 result = permutation_importance(  
2529  
clf, X[val_idx], y[val_idx],  
n_repeats=10,  
2530  
2531  
random_state=42,  
n_jobs=-1  
2532  
2533 )  
2534  
2535 # Organize rankings into a clean dataframe  
2536 importance_df = pd.DataFrame({  
2537  
'Feature': feature_pool,  
2538  
'Importance': result.importances_mean  
2539 })  
2540  
2541 # Isolate top 15 highest-scoring clinical markers for the visualization  
2542 top_features = importance_df.sort_values(by='Importance', ascending=False).head(15)  
2543  
2544 # =====================================================================  
2545 # VISUAL RENDERING: POLISHED HORIZONTAL BAR GRAPH  
2546 # =====================================================================  
2547 print("[3/3] Exporting non-interactive feature ranking asset...")  
2548  
2549 sns.set_theme(style="whitegrid", font="Segoe UI")  
2550 fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(10, 6.5), dpi=300)  
2551  
2552 # Build a clean horizontal bar layout using an elegant blue color mapping  
2553 sns.barplot(  
2554  
2555  
2556  
2557  
2558  
2559  
2560  
2561 )  
2562  
data=top_features,  
x='Importance',  
y='Feature',  
color='#1f77b4',  
edgecolor='#1a5f90',  
lw=1,  
ax=ax  
2563 # Set descriptive text boundaries  
2564 ax.set_title("Top 15 Predictive Clinical Features by Permutation Importance\n", fontsize=12,  
fontweight='bold', color='#203764')  
2565 ax.set_xlabel("\nDrop in Balanced Accuracy Score when Shuffled", fontsize=10,  
fontweight='bold')  
2566 ax.set_ylabel("Clinical Marker Feature Name\n", fontsize=10, fontweight='bold')  
2567  
2568 # Remove outer frame borders to enhance design readability  
2569 sns.despine(left=True, bottom=True)  
2570 plt.tight_layout()  
2571  
2572 # Save structural high-resolution plot asset to drive space  
2573 os.makedirs(os.path.dirname(importance_out_path), exist_ok=True)  
2574 plt.savefig(importance_out_path, bbox_inches='tight', dpi=300)  
2575 plt.close(fig)

2580 # %%  
2581 import os  
2582 import pandas as pd  
2583 import numpy as np  
2584 import matplotlib.pyplot as plt  
2585 import seaborn as sns  
2586 from sklearn.preprocessing import StandardScaler  
2587 import umap  
2588  
2589 # =====================================================================  
2590 # CONFIGURATION & DATA LOADING  
2591 # =====================================================================  
2592 csv_in = r"D:\Trial 2\Datasets I used\PPMI_Merged_Longitudinal_Output.csv"  
2593 umap_out_path = r"D:\Trial 2\Datasets I used\umap_patient_manifold.png"  
2594  
2595 print("[1/3] Loading longitudinal matrix...")  
2596 if not os.path.exists(csv_in):  
2597  
raise FileNotFoundError(f"Could not locate the dataset at {csv_in}. Run the main  
pipeline cell first.")  
2598  
2599 df = pd.read_csv(csv_in, engine="c", low_memory=False)  
2600  
2601 # =====================================================================  
2602 # MANIFOLD PREPARATION & COMPUATATION  
2603 # =====================================================================  
2604 print("[2/3] Transforming 162-dimensional clinical space via UMAP...")  
2605  
2606 feature_blacklist = {"IMPUTED_DATA", "AGE_ASSESS_JLO", "PATNO", "DIAGNOSIS", "COHORT"}  
2607 feature_pool = [c for c in df.columns if c not in feature_blacklist and not any(x in c for x  
in ["_x", "_y"])]  
2608  
2609 # Isolate features and target class arrays  
2610 X_raw = df[feature_pool].values  
2611 y = df["DIAGNOSIS"].values  
2612  
2613 # UMAP cannot accept missing values (NaNs). We impute the column means here  
2614 # strictly for the projection coordinates without affecting your core model.  
2615 from sklearn.impute import SimpleImputer  
2616 imputer = SimpleImputer(strategy='mean')  
2617 X_imputed = imputer.fit_transform(X_raw)  
2618  
2619 # CRITICAL STEP: Apply Z-score Standardization so all clinical measures  
2620 # are evaluated on an equal scale by the distance algorithm.  
2621 scaler = StandardScaler()  
2622 X_scaled = scaler.fit_transform(X_imputed)  
2623  
localhost:59932/05b8ae0c-cdd0-4ea7-8b58-a0633b3cfe36/  
55/63  
7/13/26, 5:12 PM  
Untitled-1  
2624 # Initialize and fit UMAP manifold coordinates  
2625 reducer = umap.UMAP(  
2626  
n_neighbors=30,  
min_dist=0.3,  
# Controls local vs global structure balance  
# Controls how tightly points pack together  
2627  
2628  
metric='euclidean',  
random_state=42  
2629  
2630 )  
2631 umap_embedding = reducer.fit_transform(X_scaled)  
2632  
2633 # Create a temporary DataFrame for clean plotting organization  
2634 plot_df = pd.DataFrame({  
2635  
'UMAP Dimension 1': umap_embedding[:, 0],  
2636  
'UMAP Dimension 2': umap_embedding[:, 1],  
2637  
'Diagnosis': np.where(y == 1, "Parkinson's (1)", "Control (0)")  
2638 })  
2639  
2640 # =====================================================================  
2641 # VISUAL RENDERING: POLISHED SEABORN MANIFOLD MAP  
2642 # =====================================================================  
2643 print("[3/3] Exporting high-resolution 2D UMAP scatter asset...")  
2644  
2645 sns.set_theme(style="white", font="Segoe UI")  
2646 fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(8.5, 7), dpi=300)  
2647  
2648 # Generate the manifold scatter plot with distinct clinical hue markers  
2649 sns.scatterplot(  
2650  
2651  
2652  
2653  
2654  
2655  
2656  
2657  
2658  
2659 )  
2660  
data=plot_df,  
x='UMAP Dimension 1',  
y='UMAP Dimension 2',  
hue='Diagnosis',  
palette=['#5B9BD5', '#E06666'], # Matching your standardized medical palette  
alpha=0.6,  
s=15,  
linewidth=0,  
ax=ax  
2661 # Text details and style adjustments  
2662 ax.set_title("2D UMAP Manifold Projection of Patient Phenotypes\n", fontsize=12,  
fontweight='bold', color='#203764')  
2663 ax.set_xlabel("UMAP Dimension 1", fontsize=10, fontweight='bold')  
2664 ax.set_ylabel("UMAP Dimension 2", fontsize=10, fontweight='bold')  
2665  
2666 # Clean up visual noise by removing harsh box axes rules  
2667 sns.despine(ax=ax, top=True, right=True, left=False, bottom=False)  
2668 ax.legend(title="Cohort Group", frameon=True, facecolor='white', edgecolor='none',  
loc='best')  
2669 plt.tight_layout()  
2670  
2671 # Save structural asset to disk and flush RAM  
localhost:59932/05b8ae0c-cdd0-4ea7-8b58-a0633b3cfe36/

2679 # %%  
2680 """  
2681 Feature-set novelty comparison: Clinical-only vs Digital-only vs Combined.  
2682 Run this ENTIRE file as a single block (top to bottom) in your notebook —  
2683 do not copy only part of it, or you will get NameError for missing imports.  
2684 """  
2685  
2686 # --- IMPORTS (must run first, every time) ---  
2687 import os  
2688 import pandas as pd  
2689 import numpy as np  
2690 import matplotlib.pyplot as plt  
2691 import matplotlib.ticker as mtick  
2692 from sklearn.model_selection import StratifiedKFold  
2693 from sklearn.ensemble import HistGradientBoostingClassifier  
2694  
2695 # =====================================================================  
2696 # INITIAL SETUP & CONFIGURATION  
2697 # =====================================================================  
2698 paths = {  
2699  
2700  
"diagnosis": r"D:\Trial 2\Datasets I used\Primary_Research_Diagnosis_24Jun2026.csv",  
"enrollment": r"D:\Trial 2\Datasets I used\Participant_enrollment_status__Online__1-  
2Jul2026.csv",  
"moca": r"D:\Trial 2\Datasets I used\Montreal_Cognitive_Assessment__MoCA__14F-  
eb2026.csv",  
2701  
2702  
2703  
"clock": r"D:\Trial 2\Datasets I used\Clock_Drawing_24Jun2026 (1).csv",  
"upsit": r"D:\Trial 2\Datasets I used\University_of_Pennsylvania_Smell_Identif-  
ication_Test_UPSIT_12Jul2026.csv",  
2704  
"benton": r"D:\Trial 2\Datasets I used\Benton_Judgement_of_Line_Orientation_24J-  
un2026.csv",  
2705  
2706  
"roche": r"D:\Trial 2\Datasets I used\Roche_PD_Monitoring_App_v2_data_24Jun2026.csv",  
"digital": r"D:\Trial 2\Datasets I used\Digital_Biomarker_Full_Active_TestArchiv-  
ed_24Jun2026.csv",  
2707 }  
2708  
2709 comparison_out_path = r"D:\Trial 2\Datasets I used\feature_set_comparison.png"  
2710  
2711 metadata_blacklist = {  
2712  
"INFODAT", "SITE", "ORIG_ID", "DATE_CHANGED", "REC_ID", "PPMI_ID",  
"UPDATED_AT", "CREATED_AT", "ROW_ID", "VERSION", "SURVEYNAME", "EVENT_ID",  
"UPSITORDER", "BENTONVERSION", "UPSITFORM"  
2713  
2714  
2715 }  
2716  
2717 # Which source files count as "clinical" (classic paper-and-pencil scales)

Untitled-1  
2718 # vs "digital" (app / wearable / active-test biomarkers).  
2719 CLINICAL_SOURCES = {"moca", "clock", "upsit", "benton"}  
2720 DIGITAL_SOURCES = {"roche", "digital"}  
2721  
2722  
2723 def build_dataset():  
2724

"""Runs the full merge pipeline and returns (df_final, source_of_column dict)."""  
# ---------------- STEP 1: TRUE COHORT GENERATION ----------------  
print("[1/5] Extracting true cohorts and mapping clinical definitions...")  
master_patients = pd.DataFrame(columns=["PATNO"])  
for label_file in ["diagnosis", "enrollment"]:  
if not os.path.exists(paths[label_file]):  
continue  
df = pd.read_csv(paths[label_file], engine="c", low_memory=False)  
df.columns = [c.upper().strip() for c in df.columns]  
if "PATNO" not in df.columns:  
continue  
df["PATNO"] = df["PATNO"].astype(str).str.strip().str.lstrip("0")  
rename_rules = {}  
for c in df.columns:  
if any(x in c for x in ["DIAG", "DX", "PRIMDIAG"]) and c != "DIAGNOSIS":  
rename_rules[c] = "DIAGNOSIS"  
elif any(x in c for x in ["COHORT", "SUBSET", "STUDY"]) and c != "COHORT":  
rename_rules[c] = "COHORT"  
if rename_rules:  
df = df.rename(columns=rename_rules)  
df = df.loc[:, ~df.columns.duplicated()]  
keep_cols = [c for c in ["PATNO", "DIAGNOSIS", "COHORT"] if c in df.columns]  
sub_labels = df[keep_cols].dropna(subset=["PATNO"])  
if master_patients.empty:  
master_patients = sub_labels  
else:  
master_patients = pd.merge(master_patients, sub_labels, on="PATNO", how="outer",  
suffixes=("", "_ALT"))  
master_patients = master_patients.loc[:, ~master_patients.columns.duplicated()]  
for col in ["DIAGNOSIS", "COHORT"]:  
alt_col = f"{col}_ALT"  
if alt_col in master_patients.columns:  
master_patients[col] = master_patients[col].fillna(master_patients[alt_col])  
master_patients = master_patients.drop(columns=[alt_col])  
def resolve_ppmi_status(row):  
if "COHORT" in row and pd.notna(row["COHORT"]):

c_val = str(row["COHORT"]).upper()  
if any(w in c_val for w in ["HEALTHY", "CONTROL", "HC"]):  
return 0  
if any(w in c_val for w in ["PARKINSON", "PD"]):  
return 1  
if "DIAGNOSIS" in row and pd.notna(row["DIAGNOSIS"]):  
try:  
d_val = float(row["DIAGNOSIS"])  
if d_val == 1.0:  
return 1  
if d_val in [2.0, 17.0, 25.0, 23.0]:  
return 0  
except ValueError:  
d_str = str(row["DIAGNOSIS"]).upper()  
if any(w in d_str for w in ["CONTROL", "HEALTHY"]):  
return 0  
if any(w in d_str for w in ["PD", "PARKINSON"]):  
return 1  
return -1  
master_patients["FINAL_TARGET"] = master_patients.apply(resolve_ppmi_status, axis=1)  
master_patients = master_patients[master_patients["FINAL_TARGET"].isin([0, 1])]  
master_patients = master_patients.drop(columns=["DIAGNOSIS"], errors="ignore").rename(  
columns={"FINAL_TARGET": "DIAGNOSIS"})  
master_patients = master_patients.drop_duplicates(subset=["PATNO"])  
class_counts = master_patients["DIAGNOSIS"].value_counts()  
print(f"  
-> {class_counts.get(1, 0)} PD vs {class_counts.get(0, 0)} Control.")  
# ---------------- STEP 2: SOURCE-TAGGED HORIZONTAL PROCESSING ----------------  
# Every feature column is prefixed with its source file, e.g. "MOCA__MOCATOTAL".  
# This makes clinical-vs-digital splitting exact, regardless of any incidental  
# name collisions between files (your original suffix logic only tagged  
# columns that collided, which is unreliable for this kind of grouping).  
print("[2/5] Compiling source-tagged patient analytics matrices...")  
feature_dfs = []  
source_of_column = {} # column_name -> "clinical" or "digital"  
for name, path in paths.items():  
if name in ["diagnosis", "enrollment"] or not os.path.exists(path):  
continue  
df = pd.read_csv(path, engine="c", low_memory=False)  
df.columns = [c.upper().strip() for c in df.columns]  
if "PATNO" not in df.columns:  
continue  
df["PATNO"] = df["PATNO"].astype(str).str.strip().str.lstrip("0")  
cols_to_drop = [

for col in df.columns:  
if col != "PATNO":  
df[col] = pd.to_numeric(df[col], errors='coerce')  
df_collapsed = df.groupby("PATNO", as_index=False).mean()  
rename_map = {c: f"{name.upper()}__{c}" for c in df_collapsed.columns if c !=  
df_collapsed = df_collapsed.rename(columns=rename_map)

group = "clinical" if name in CLINICAL_SOURCES else "digital"  
for new_col in rename_map.values():  
source_of_column[new_col] = group  
feature_dfs.append(df_collapsed)  
df_final = master_patients.copy()  
for f_df in feature_dfs:  
df_final = pd.merge(df_final, f_df, on="PATNO", how="left")  
# ---------------- STEP 3: FEATURE ENGINEERING + LEAK-FREE FILTER ----------------  
print("[3/5] Running feature synthesis and validity filters...")  
scent_cols = [c for c in df_final.columns if "SCENT_" in c and "_CORRECT" in c]  
if scent_cols:  
df_final["UPSIT__ENGINEERED_TOTAL_SCENT_SCORE"] = df_final[scent_cols].sum(axis=1,  
min_count=1)

2865  
source_of_column["UPSIT__ENGINEERED_TOTAL_SCENT_SCORE"] = "clinical"  
feature_blacklist = {"IMPUTED_DATA", "AGE_ASSESS_JLO"}  
all_feature_cols = []  
for col in list(df_final.columns):  
if col in ["PATNO", "DIAGNOSIS", "COHORT"]:  
continue  
if any(b in col for b in feature_blacklist):  
df_final = df_final.drop(columns=[col])  
continue  
valid_entries = df_final[col].notna().sum()  
if valid_entries >= 15:  
all_feature_cols.append(col)  
else:  
df_final = df_final.drop(columns=[col])

-> {len(all_feature_cols)} total leak-free features retained.")  
return df_final, all_feature_cols, source_of_column  
2870 def run_comparison(df_final, all_feature_cols, source_of_column):  
2871  
2872  
2873  
2874  
clinical_cols = [c for c in all_feature_cols if source_of_column.get(c) == "clinical"]  
digital_cols = [c for c in all_feature_cols if source_of_column.get(c) == "digital"]  
print(f"  
features "  
-> {len(clinical_cols)} clinical features | {len(digital_cols)} digital

2914  
f"| {len(all_feature_cols)} combined")  
feature_sets = {  
"Clinical-only\n(UPSIT/MoCA/Benton/Clock)": clinical_cols,  
"Digital-only\n(Roche app + active tests)": digital_cols,  
"Combined\n(Clinical + Digital)": all_feature_cols,  
}
# ---------------- STEP 4: CROSS-VALIDATED EVALUATION ----------------  
print("[4/5] Running 5-fold cross-validation for each feature set...")  
y = df_final["DIAGNOSIS"].values  
cv = StratifiedKFold(n_splits=5, shuffle=True, random_state=42)  
results = []  
for label, cols in feature_sets.items():  
if len(cols) == 0:  
print(f"  
-> Skipping '{label}': no features available.")  
continue  
X = df_final[cols].values  
fold_bal_acc, fold_sens, fold_spec = [], [], []  
for train_idx, test_idx in cv.split(X, y):  
clf = HistGradientBoostingClassifier(  
loss='log_loss',  
learning_rate=0.015,  
max_iter=300,  
max_leaf_nodes=40,  
min_samples_leaf=30,  
l2_regularization=0.2,  
class_weight='balanced',  
early_stopping=True,  
n_iter_no_change=12,  
random_state=42  
)
clf.fit(X[train_idx], y[train_idx])  
preds = clf.predict(X[test_idx])  
y_test = y[test_idx]  
localhost:59932/05b8ae0c-cdd0-4ea7-8b58-a0633b3cfe36/  
61/63  
7/13/26, 5:12 PM  
Untitled-1  
tp = np.sum((preds == 1) & (y_test == 1))  
2915  
2916  
2917  
2918  
2919  
2920  
2921  
2922  
2923  
2924  
2925  
2926

2946

2949  
2950  
2951  
2952  
2953  
2954  
2955  
2956  
tn = np.sum((preds == 0) & (y_test == 0))  
fp = np.sum((preds == 1) & (y_test == 0))  
fn = np.sum((preds == 0) & (y_test == 1))  
sens = tp / (tp + fn) if (tp + fn) > 0 else 0  
spec = tn / (tn + fp) if (tn + fp) > 0 else 0  
fold_bal_acc.append((sens + spec) / 2)  
fold_sens.append(sens)  
fold_spec.append(spec)  
results.append({  
"Feature Set": label,  
"N Features": len(cols),  
"Balanced Accuracy": np.mean(fold_bal_acc),  
"Sensitivity (PD)": np.mean(fold_sens),  
"Specificity (Control)": np.mean(fold_spec),  
})  
print(f"  
-> {label.splitlines()[0]}: "  
f"BalAcc={np.mean(fold_bal_acc):.4f} Sens={np.mean(fold_sens):.4f} "  
f"Spec={np.mean(fold_spec):.4f}")  
results_df = pd.DataFrame(results)  
print("\n--- Feature Set Comparison Table ---")  
print(results_df.to_string(index=False))  
# ---------------- STEP 5: GROUPED BAR CHART ----------------  
print("\n[5/5] Rendering comparison chart...")  
metrics = ["Balanced Accuracy", "Sensitivity (PD)", "Specificity (Control)"]  
labels = [r["Feature Set"] for r in results]  
x = np.arange(len(labels))  
width = 0.25  
colors = ["#1f77b4", "#5B9BD5", "#E06666"]  
plt.style.use('seaborn-v0_8-whitegrid')  
fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(9, 6), dpi=300)  
for i, metric in enumerate(metrics):  
values = [r[metric] for r in results]  
bars = ax.bar(x + (i - 1) * width, values, width, label=metric, color=colors[i],  
edgecolor='white')  
for bar, v in zip(bars, values):  
2957  
2958  
2959  
2960  
2961  
2962

ax.text(bar.get_x() + bar.get_width() / 2, v + 0.01, f"{v:.2f}",

print("\nInterpretation guide:")  
print(" - If 'Digital-only' beats 'Clinical-only': the app/active-test data carries real  
signal on its own.")  
2981  
print(" - If 'Combined' clearly beats both individually: digital biomarkers add  
incremental value")  
2982  
2983  
print(" on top of standard clinical scales -> this is your novelty claim.")  
print(" - If 'Combined' ~= 'Clinical-only': the digital data isn't adding anything once  
you already")  
2984  
print(" have UPSIT/MoCA/Benton -> still a valid, honest finding, just a different  
one.")
